metadata.title: Research Venetoclax: What Is BH3 Profiling and Why Does It Predict Response?
metadata.color_semantics: teardown: INK #2A1A0E body text; CRIMSON #C8102E key finding/vulnerability; SLATE #545454 structure/neutral
metadata.note: CLI video REQUIRED spine: B00 intro · B01 PROBLEM · B02 ASK · B03 CODE · B04 OUTPUT(slate) · B05 CHANGE · B06 OUTPUT(slate) · B07 SUMMARY · B08 NEXT STEPS · B09 outro. Source: cancer-biology/chapters/14-apoptosis-in-cancer-evasion-and-restoration.md

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. BH3 profiling — the biology is solved.

lines: BH3 profiling — the biology is solved.

beats.narration_text: The oncologist cannot predict which CLL patient will achieve minimal residual disease negativity on venetoclax. BH3 profiling claims to solve this — but it is not standard of care. Why not? The assay works. The barriers are fresh cells, specialized flow cytometry, and rapid turnaround. The biology is solved. The delivery system is not.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- problem card: venetoclax prediction gap; BH3 profiling as solution not yet implemented

beats.narration_text: In the terminal: ask Claude to research BH3 profiling as a predictive biomarker — assay methodology, clinical evidence, and barriers.

props.command:
claude "Research BH3 profiling as a predictive biomarker
for venetoclax response. What is the assay
methodology? What published studies correlate BH3
profiling with clinical outcomes in CLL and AML?
What are the barriers to routine clinical
implementation, and are any trials incorporating
it as a companion diagnostic?"

beats.role_note: terminal (Onda) -- Python script reading Claude response
beats.narration_text: Read the code. The script builds a predictive versus prognostic distinction table from Claude's response — assay method, predictive studies cited, and the barriers column. The distinction matters clinically: predictive tells you who responds, prognostic tells you how bad the disease is.

props.code:
# bh3_profile_audit.py -- Claude Code output
ASSAY = {
  "method": "BH3 peptide + flow cytometry (MOMP)",
  "cell_requirement": "fresh (cannot fix/freeze)",
  "turnaround": "same-day",
  "predictive_studies": [
    "Vo 2012 Science (AML, n=34)",
    "Bhatt 2020 NEJM Evid (CLL, n=48)",
  ],
  "barriers": [
    "Fresh cells required",
    "Specialized flow cytometry lab",
    "Same-day processing",
  ],
  "companion_dx_status": "No FDA companion dx yet",
}
print(f"Method: {ASSAY['method']}")
print(f"Predictive studies: {len(ASSAY['predictive_studies'])}")
for b in ASSAY['barriers']:
  print(f"  BARRIER: {b}")
print(f"Status: {ASSAY['companion_dx_status']}")

beats.narration_text: The decision memo prints. BH3 profiling predicts venetoclax response in two published studies. Assay: BH3 peptide added to live tumor cells, mitochondrial outer membrane permeabilization measured by flow cytometry. Three barriers: fresh cells required, specialized flow lab needed, same-day processing. No FDA companion diagnostic as of 2026.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- screen-recording: decision memo printed: assay summary, predictive evidence, three barriers to clinical adoption

beats.narration_text: Now ask whether the same logic applies to MCL-1, the next target in the BCL-2 family.

props.command:
claude "Does any analogous functional assay to BH3
profiling exist for MCL-1 inhibitors? What is
the clinical development status of MCL-1
inhibitors versus venetoclax?"

beats.narration_text: MCL-1 comparison. No analogous functional assay exists for MCL-1 inhibitors. MCL-1 inhibitors — AMG-176, AZD5991 — are in Phase 1 to 2 trials with dose-limiting cardiac toxicity. Venetoclax is approved for CLL and AML. MCL-1 inhibitors are years behind, and no biomarker equivalent to BH3 profiling has been developed.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- screen-recording: MCL-1 comparison table; no companion diagnostic equivalent found

beats.narration_text: The assay works scientifically. The barrier is logistics, not biology. The biology is solved — the assay correctly predicts which cells are primed for apoptosis when BCL-2 is blocked. The delivery system is not solved — fresh cells, specialized flow, and same-day turnaround are not available at most cancer centers.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- summary card: biology solved / logistics not; two-column contrast

beats.narration_text: Find the Letai group's BH3 profiling protocol and check whether any cancer center near you has implemented it as a clinical decision tool. The Dana-Farber Cancer Institute has published their protocol. If your center does not have it, ask why — the answer is usually logistics, not evidence.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next steps card: find Letai protocol, check local implementation

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
